jobs.name: github-bounty-income-30m
jobs.prompt:
Run deterministic GitHub bounty income pipeline with stages: Scout + Score + Repo Analysis Gate + Report. This is a no-agent cron script job. It may clone or fetch candidate repositories into .hermes/bounty-workspace for inspection and reporting only. It must not claim bounties, create branches, commit, push, create PRs, or make code changes unless the user has explicitly confirmed a TAKE target in this thread. After TAKE confirmation, code changes may be prepared locally only; claiming/reserving, branch creation, commit, push, and PR creation still require separate explicit user instruction. It may add GitHub issue/PR comments only when explicitly part of a safe reporting workflow. When a high-value TAKE bounty is identified or a comment is successfully added, trigger: osascript -e 'display notification "Found/Commented on Bounty" with title "Hermes Bounty Alert"'.

Use only actual available workflows. In this no-agent script, agent skills are unavailable, so use deterministic GitHub CLI search, issue inspection, local scoring, local clone/fetch, watchlist inspection, and report generation. Scout broadly across Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go using GitHub CLI labels/terms: bounty, paid, reward, sponsor, good first issue, help wanted, and explicit amounts. Also inspect .hermes/config/bounty-watchlist.json every run for read-only tracking targets such as Kyo gRPC #390, ISAAC-497 Enhanced RAG Pipeline, and Twenty IMAP. Collect title, url, repository, labels, updatedAt, createdAt, commentsCount, body, comments, and existing PR signals when available.

Filter and rank aggressively. Exclude radar/artifact aggregation repos, duplicate claim issues, stale or vague tasks without maintainer authorization, private vulnerability requests that should not be public, projects without clear payout/acceptance terms, crowded duplicate-PR races, and anything requiring unauthorized scanning or exploit activity. TAKE requires clear payout terms, acceptance criteria, active maintainer/project signals, low legal/security risk, plausible implementation path, and likely local verification path. SKIP/WATCH must return DONT_NOTIFY plus wakeAgent=false and write a local report. TAKE must output target repo, issue URL, language, bounty/terms evidence, legitimacy reasoning, smallest viable contribution, likely files if known, tests, safe submission path, validation checklist, rollback/debug path, and must notify macOS. Manual watchlist targets must be reported with repo, issue/bounty link, amount, current status, required skills, estimated difficulty, competition risk, existing PR signal, recommended action, reason, and next safe read-only command.

Never claim or imply guaranteed income. Never run unauthorized scanning, spam comments, disclose private vulnerabilities publicly, fabricate findings, bypass platform rules, or publish payout-sensitive data.
